description: Fix existing issues in The Living Word Bible app: light mode readability, add Wikipedia images/references, fix overlapping/alignment issues, and production hardening

stories.title: Fix Hero Section for Light & Sepia Themes
stories.description:
The HeroSection has hardcoded dark overlay colors (from-[#0F0F1A]/80 via-[#0F0F1A]/70 to-[#0F0F1A]/95) that don't adapt to light/sepia themes, making text nearly invisible in light mode. The MapPreview section also has a hardcoded dark gradient background (from-[#1a1f2e] via-[#1e2438] to-[#151927]) and hardcoded grid pattern colors that ignore theme.

Fixes needed:
1. In components/landing/HeroSection.tsx: Replace hardcoded #0F0F1A overlay with CSS variable-based overlay that works across all 3 themes. For light mode use a light overlay with sufficient contrast for gold/white text. Consider using theme-aware classes or CSS variables for the gradient.
2. In components/landing/MapPreview.tsx: Replace the hardcoded dark gradient background with theme-aware CSS variables. The grid pattern overlay also uses hardcoded gold rgba - make it theme-aware.
3. Ensure the hero text (gold heading, secondary text, verse quote, muted ref) all have sufficient contrast (WCAG AA minimum) against backgrounds in ALL three themes.
4. The 'Begin Reading' CTA button uses bg-gold with text-[var(--bg-primary)] - verify this has sufficient contrast in light mode where bg-primary is very light (#FDFBF7) and gold is dark (#8B6914).

Existing files to modify:
- components/landing/HeroSection.tsx
- components/landing/MapPreview.tsx
- app/globals.css (if new CSS variables needed for overlays)

Acceptance criteria:
- Hero section is fully readable in dark, light, and sepia themes
- Map preview section adapts to all themes
- All text meets WCAG AA contrast ratios
- No hardcoded dark colors remain in these components

stories.title: Fix Light Mode Text Contrast Across All Pages
stories.description:
Audit and fix text contrast issues across the entire app in light mode. Key areas:

1. In app/globals.css: The light theme --text-muted (#8A8278) and --text-secondary (#5C5346) may have insufficient contrast on --bg-primary (#FDFBF7). Verify and adjust. The --verse-number (#8B7D6B) is the same across all themes - needs to be darker for light mode.
2. Section headings using 'text-gold' class - in light mode gold is #8B6914 on white bg (#FDFBF7) - verify contrast ratio, may need darkening.
3. The BooksGrid component uses text-[var(--text-muted)] for abbreviations and text-[var(--text-primary)] for book names - verify readability.
4. TimelinePreview uses text-[10px] and text-[11px] sizes with muted colors - extremely hard to read in light mode.
5. EvidenceSpotlight card badges use bg-[var(--bg-primary)]/80 with gold text - in light mode this is nearly-white-on-gold, illegible.
6. SearchSection placeholder text and hint text contrast.
7. Footer uses text-muted-foreground extensively - verify in light mode.

Existing files to modify:
- app/globals.css (adjust light theme CSS variables)
- components/landing/BooksGrid.tsx (if class changes needed)
- components/landing/TimelinePreview.tsx (increase font sizes for small text)
- components/landing/EvidenceSpotlight.tsx (fix badge contrast)

Acceptance criteria:
- All text on all landing page sections is clearly readable in light mode
- WCAG AA contrast ratios met for all text
- Small text (10-11px) is either enlarged or given stronger contrast
- Category badges on evidence cards are legible in all themes

stories.title: Add Wikipedia Images to Landing Page Sections
stories.description:
Several landing page sections lack images or use placeholder representations. Add real Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons images:

1. MapPreview (components/landing/MapPreview.tsx): Currently uses a plain dark gradient with pin dots. Replace with an actual historical biblical map image from Wikimedia Commons as background. Good options: 'https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/90/Palestine_1020BC-Smith1915.jpg/800px-Palestine_1020BC-Smith1915.jpg' or similar ancient map of the Holy Land. Keep the pins and CTA overlay.

2. TimelinePreview (components/landing/TimelinePreview.tsx): The timeline cards have no images. Add small representative Wikipedia images to at least the major events. Add an imageUrl field to the TimelineEvent interface and populate with appropriate Wikimedia Commons URLs:
   - Call of Abraham: Abraham painting
   - The Exodus: Moses/Red Sea painting
   - David Becomes King: David painting
   - Solomon's Temple: Temple illustration
   - Birth of Jesus: Nativity painting
   - Crucifixion: Crucifixion painting
   Use Next.js Image component with proper alt text and attribution.

3. BooksGrid (components/landing/BooksGrid.tsx): Add a subtle background image or decorative element - perhaps an ancient manuscript texture from Wikimedia.

Existing files to modify:
- components/landing/MapPreview.tsx
- components/landing/TimelinePreview.tsx
- components/landing/BooksGrid.tsx

Acceptance criteria:
- MapPreview has a real historical map as background
- Timeline events show relevant artwork thumbnails
- All images use Next.js Image with proper alt text
- Images load from upload.wikimedia.org (already in remotePatterns)
- Attribution text included where visible

stories.title: Add Wikipedia Images to Evidence & People Pages
stories.description:
The evidence and people data in the database may have missing or placeholder image URLs. Ensure real Wikipedia images are populated and displayed correctly.

1. Check the seed data in data/evidence.json - verify all evidence items have valid Wikimedia image URLs. If any are missing, add appropriate URLs:
   - Dead Sea Scrolls, Tel Dan Stele, Pilate Stone already have URLs in the fallback data
   - Check the database entries match

2. Check data/people.json - verify people entries have image URLs. Add Wikipedia portrait images for major biblical figures:
   - Moses, David, Solomon, Abraham, Jesus, Paul, Peter, Mary, etc.
   - Use famous paintings from Wikimedia Commons (Rembrandt, Caravaggio, Raphael, etc.)

3. In components/people/PersonCard.tsx - ensure it displays the image if available, with proper fallback/placeholder for people without images.

4. In components/evidence/EvidenceCard.tsx - ensure images are displayed with Next.js Image component properly.

5. Add proper Wikimedia attribution/source links where images are displayed.

Existing files to modify:
- data/evidence.json (add/verify image URLs)
- data/people.json (add image URLs for major figures)
- components/people/PersonCard.tsx (ensure image display)
- components/evidence/EvidenceCard.tsx (verify image display)
- lib/db/seed.ts (if seed needs to be re-run with updated data)

Acceptance criteria:
- All evidence items display real archaeological photos from Wikipedia
- Major biblical people show relevant artwork/portraits
- Proper attribution on all images
- Fallback state for people without images is visually clean

stories.title: Fix Header, Mobile Nav & Content Overlap Issues
stories.description:
Fix overlapping and alignment issues across the app:

1. Header overlap: The header is sticky (sticky top-0 z-40) but the hero section starts at h-[100dvh] from the very top. This means the header overlaps the hero content. The hero content needs a top padding or the layout needs adjusting so content doesn't hide behind the sticky header. On non-hero pages, content should also not be hidden behind the header.

2. Mobile navigation (MobileNav.tsx): The SheetContent doesn't specify a side prop - verify it defaults correctly and doesn't overlap with other UI elements. The mobile nav has pt-12 which may not account for the close button properly.

3. BooksGrid alignment: The grid uses xl:grid-cols-11 which is an unusual column count that may cause the last row to look unbalanced. On mobile (grid-cols-2) with 66 books, layout should be clean.

4. TimelinePreview: The horizontal line (absolute top-[38px]) may not align with the dots on different screen sizes or font scaling. The timeline cards and dots need to be properly aligned.

5. EvidenceSpotlight carousel: The -mx-4 px-4 trick for edge-to-edge scrolling may cause horizontal overflow issues or misalignment with the header/title above.

6. Footer grid: On tablet/medium screens, the 3-column grid may create awkward wrapping. The version selector max-w-[200px] inside a grid column may look disconnected.

Existing files to modify:
- app/layout.tsx (header/content spacing)
- components/landing/HeroSection.tsx (account for header height)
- components/layout/MobileNav.tsx (verify sheet positioning)
- components/landing/BooksGrid.tsx (grid column optimization)
- components/landing/TimelinePreview.tsx (dot/line alignment)
- components/landing/EvidenceSpotlight.tsx (overflow fix)
- components/layout/Footer.tsx (responsive grid)

Acceptance criteria:
- No content hidden behind sticky header on any page
- Hero section properly accounts for header height
- Mobile nav opens without overlapping issues
- Books grid looks balanced on all screen sizes
- Timeline dots and line are perfectly aligned
- No horizontal overflow on any screen size
- Footer looks good at all breakpoints

stories.title: Fix Bible Reader & Context Panel Alignment
stories.description:
Fix overlapping and alignment issues specific to the Bible reader pages:

1. Check app/bible/[bookSlug]/[chapter]/page.tsx - the chapter reader layout with sidebar context panel. Ensure the main content area properly shrinks when the sidebar is open on desktop, and doesn't overlap.

2. Check components/layout/Sidebar.tsx - verify the sidebar (380px width) doesn't overlap main content on medium screens (768-1024px). May need to switch from overlay to push layout, or reduce width on smaller screens.

3. Check components/scripture/ContextPanel.tsx - ensure tabs don't overflow on mobile.

4. Check ChapterToolbar.tsx (app/bible/ChapterToolbar.tsx) - the toolbar with reading mode toggle and font size controls should not overlap with navigation or chapter title.

5. Cross-reference popovers (CrossRefPopover.tsx) - verify they don't go off-screen on mobile or near viewport edges.

6. Dictionary tooltips (DictionaryTooltip.tsx) - verify positioning works correctly and doesn't create overflow.

7. Chapter navigation (ChapterNavigation.tsx) - prev/next buttons should be properly aligned and not overlap with content.

Existing files to modify:
- app/bible/[bookSlug]/[chapter]/page.tsx
- components/layout/Sidebar.tsx
- components/scripture/ContextPanel.tsx
- app/bible/ChapterToolbar.tsx
- components/scripture/CrossRefPopover.tsx
- components/dictionary/DictionaryTooltip.tsx
- components/scripture/ChapterNavigation.tsx

Acceptance criteria:
- Sidebar doesn't overlap content on any screen size
- Context panel tabs are fully accessible on mobile
- Toolbar controls don't overlap with each other
- Popovers and tooltips stay within viewport bounds
- Chapter navigation is cleanly positioned

stories.title: Fix Dead Links & Missing Pages
stories.description:
Fix broken links and create missing pages that are referenced but don't exist:

1. The ArtworkOfDay component links to '/gallery' which doesn't exist. Either:
   a. Create a basic gallery page at app/gallery/page.tsx that displays artwork from the database, OR
   b. Change the link to point to an existing page (like /bible or /evidence)
   Recommendation: Create a simple gallery page since artwork data exists in the media table.

2. The Footer links to '/privacy' which likely doesn't exist. Create a minimal privacy policy page at app/privacy/page.tsx.

3. Verify all navigation links in NAV_LINKS (components/layout/nav-links.ts) point to existing pages.

4. Verify all internal links on the landing page work: /bible, /maps, /evidence, /timeline, /search, /about.

5. Check for any 404s from evidence/[slug] or people/[slug] links.

Existing files to modify:
- components/landing/ArtworkOfDay.tsx (fix gallery link)
- components/layout/nav-links.ts (verify all links)

New files to create:
- app/gallery/page.tsx (artwork gallery page)
- app/privacy/page.tsx (privacy policy page)

Acceptance criteria:
- No dead links anywhere in the app
- Gallery page exists and shows artwork
- Privacy page exists with basic content
- All nav links verified working

stories.title: Production Polish: Error States, Loading, SEO & Performance
stories.description:
Final production hardening pass:

1. Error handling: Create app/not-found.tsx and app/error.tsx with themed, branded error pages that match the museum aesthetic.

2. Loading states: Create app/loading.tsx with a skeleton/spinner that matches the gold theme. Also add loading.tsx for key dynamic routes: app/bible/[bookSlug]/[chapter]/loading.tsx, app/evidence/loading.tsx, app/people/loading.tsx.

3. Add proper favicon: Check if favicon.ico exists in app/ directory. If not, create one or add a link to the existing og-image.

4. Verify SEO: Run through the sitemap.ts and ensure all routes are included. Verify robots.ts allows indexing.

5. Performance: Add loading='lazy' to below-fold images on the landing page (ArtworkOfDay, EvidenceSpotlight images). The HeroSection image should have priority={true}.

6. Accessibility: Ensure all interactive elements have visible focus indicators in all themes. Verify skip-to-content link works.

7. Build verification: Run `npm run build` to ensure zero errors. Fix any TypeScript or build errors.

Existing files to modify:
- components/landing/ArtworkOfDay.tsx (lazy loading)
- components/landing/EvidenceSpotlight.tsx (lazy loading)
- components/landing/HeroSection.tsx (priority image loading)

New files to create:
- app/not-found.tsx
- app/error.tsx
- app/loading.tsx
- app/bible/[bookSlug]/[chapter]/loading.tsx

Acceptance criteria:
- Custom 404 and error pages with themed design
- Loading skeletons for major routes
- `npm run build` completes with zero errors
- All images have proper loading strategy (priority vs lazy)
- Focus indicators visible in all themes
- No console errors or warnings in browser
